query: Wang Yi China Foreign Minister visit South Korea 2026 postponed Taiwan dispute rescheduled July August 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my key findings on Wang Yi's postponed South Korea visit:

**Current Status:** Wang Yi's planned visit to South Korea remains **stalled/on hold with no confirmed rescheduled date**. As of the latest direct reporting (April 2026), Korea's MOFA said no arrangements were finalized. **No confirmed July or August 2026 Seoul visit was found** — Wang Yi's actual confirmed early-July travel was to the Nordic countries (July 2–8, 2026).

**The Taiwan Dispute:** The delay traces to South Korea's electronic arrival card labeling Taiwan as "Taiwan (China)"/"China (Taiwan)." After Taiwan objected, Seoul removed the departure/destination fields entirely on March 31, 2026. China then objected (April 14 MFA statement), reportedly putting the visit on hold. Seoul downplayed the linkage as a "simple administrative adjustment." China also reportedly blocked a South Korean intelligence chief's visit around the same time.

**Broader Context:** Despite this friction, China–ROK ties are in a "reset" phase — Lee Jae-myung visited Beijing Jan 5, 2026 (15 agreements signed), following the Nov 2025 Xi-Lee APEC summit in Gyeongju. Wang Yi's spring/summer 2026 focus notably tilted toward North Korea instead (Pyongyang visits in April and June).

**Important caveat:** The most detailed reporting dates to April 2026; I found no May–July confirmation of either rescheduling or formal cancellation, so the situation may have evolved beyond the available data vintage.

key_facts: Wang Yi's planned visit to South Korea was put on hold/stalled and, as of April 2026, had no finalized dates and was not rescheduled.
No confirmed Wang Yi visit to Seoul in July or August 2026 was found; his confirmed early-July 2026 travel was to Denmark, Sweden, Finland, Norway (July 2-8, 2026).
The dispute originated from South Korea's electronic arrival card labeling Taiwan as 'Taiwan (China)'/'China (Taiwan)'.
On March 31, 2026, South Korea removed the 'previous departure place' and 'next destination' fields entirely from the e-arrival card, ending the contested labeling.
China objected: a Chinese MFA spokesperson said on April 14, 2026 that Taiwan is part of China and 'China (Taiwan)' labeling is 'entirely natural'.
South Korea's MOFA (April 20, 2026) said no arrangements were finalized and characterized the card revision as 'a simple administrative and technical adjustment' unrelated to scheduling.
China reportedly also blocked/refused a visit by South Korea's National Intelligence Service director amid the tensions (UPI, April 21, 2026).
Broader China-ROK ties are in a 'reset' phase: President Lee Jae-myung visited Beijing Jan 5, 2026 (15 cooperation documents signed), following the Nov 1, 2025 Xi-Lee APEC summit in Gyeongju.
Wang Yi's April-June 2026 focus tilted toward North Korea: Pyongyang visit April 9-10 and accompanying Xi's DPRK state visit June 8-9, 2026.
Seoul sought Wang's visit to coordinate on Korean Peninsula issues ahead of an anticipated Trump-Xi summit and as follow-up to the January Lee-Xi summit.

data_points.metric: Date South Korea removed departure/destination fields from e-arrival card
Date Chinese MFA objected to 'China (Taiwan)' labeling
Date Korea MOFA said no Wang Yi visit arrangements finalized
Cooperation documents signed at Jan 2026 Lee-Xi Beijing summit
Wang Yi Nordic tour dates (Denmark, Sweden, Finland, Norway)
